summary: Crisp sound‑board recording of a summer‑93 show highlighted by a legendary Jack Straw > Foolish Heart opener, soaring Eyes and unforgettable Corrina.
review: The performance is widely praised for its tight playing, magical second set (Eyes, So Many Roads, Brokedown Palace) and the iconic Jack Straw/Foolish Heart opener.  The sound‑board source is generally clear and forward‑looking, though a few listeners note occasional thinness and mix jitter that keep it from being flawless.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews (average 4 ★) focus on strong musical moments and a mostly clean sound‑board tape; the few technical complaints are outweighed by the enthusiastic show appraisal.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers describe the mix as crisp, delightful and forward‑facing. A minority mention thin mids, muffled sections, occasional drop‑outs and “mix jitter” that cause brief listening discomfort.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set opens with the legendary Jack Straw/Foolish Heart pairing, maintains a relaxed yet jam‑heavy groove through the first set and erupts into an ecstatic second set anchored by The Eyes and So Many Roads, ending on a sweet Brokedown Palace.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for tone, guitar solos and stage presence (big wave goodbye, soulful Roads, soaring solo work).
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as clean, upfront in the mix; several reviewers call his playing “fucking on” and a driving force during jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Complimented for tight rhythm work and memorable riffs (especially the ending riff of Foolish Heart).
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince receives both love (“beautiful trills”, “shimmering stuff”) and criticism (“throwaway Vince showcase”). Overall, keyboards are highlighted as a key texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums/Space praised for energy; a few comments mention thin drum sound in the left/right channels.
